title: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva
wikitext:
[]: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by GAP » Sat Jul 04, 2009 10:00 pm This kind of pertains to Eva as well as other mecha of this type but why are the pilots required to be hot blooded and irrational like we see in Asuka. Granted, Asuka as a person is somewhat unbearable and Asuka herself is basically driven by the need to be the best there but is required in most super robot anime that the characters should be that? I don't know whether it is the idealistic hero thing or just plain tradition but Eva a shows that may not always be a good idea to have that kind of pilot and deconstructs quite violently this trope. Again, I ask why it required to have pilots like Asuka?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jul 04, 2009 10:03 pm mama, mama oh, and go look up Freud and Oedipus Complex for good measure

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Sat Jul 04, 2009 10:05 pm Because non hot blooded piloting and mecha often is not as interesting to watch.

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Jul 04, 2009 10:25 pm GAP wrote: Again, I ask why it required to have pilots like Asuka?

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Sat Jul 04, 2009 11:37 pm GAP wrote: This kind of pertains to Eva as well as other mecha of this type but why are the pilots required to be hot blooded and irrational like we see in Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Sat Jul 04, 2009 11:44 pm Because, in a gundam there's no room for Yui to act when Shinji is being owned.

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 05, 2009 2:39 am GAP wrote: This kind of pertains to Eva as well as other mecha of this type but why are the pilots required to be hot blooded and irrational like we see in Asuka.

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 05, 2009 3:24 am GAP wrote: why are the pilots required to be hot blooded?

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Jul 05, 2009 12:34 pm Mhm, I'll just stick to Hall's answer. Mr. Tines wrote: Actually, TTGL aside, you don't have to have all the pilots be hot-blooded -- as the legendary copypasta reminds us The secret of Evangelion is that they took super robot pilots and reversed their genders if Rei was male, he'd be the awesomely silent/cool/calm guy...

[]: Re: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Jul 05, 2009 2:18 pm It should also be remembered that Eva wasn't the first show to do this sort of genre subversion, it merely went way further in it than its predesessors. Zambot 3, the original Gundam and Ideon made a big deal about how piloting a giant robot sucks and will quite likely get you killed. The main pilots of Voltes V and Gunbuster found their inner resolve through A LOT of angsting. And of course there's the whole character archetype of a gritty "cold-blooded" veteran pilot (supposedly Votoms is a shining example of this, but I haven't seen it yet myself). There are also different styles of hot-bloodedness, obviously. Irrationality is by no means a mandatory requirement (although it sure can be entertaining).
summary: []: Hot Blooded pilots and Eva             Quote Post by GAP » Sat Jul 04, 2009 10:00 pm This kind of pertains to Eva as well as other mecha of this type but why are the pilots required to be hot blooded and irrational like we see in Asuka. Granted, Asuka as a person is somewhat unbearable and Asuka herself is basically driven by the need to be the best there but is required in most super robot anime that the characters should be that? I do
